sentences.text: Det er min første dag i København.
sentences.translation: It's my first day in Copenhagen.

words.grammar: første · ordinal of én (one)

sentences.text: Jeg står på hovedbanegården og ser mig omkring.
sentences.translation: I'm standing at the main train station and looking around.

words.grammar: hoved (main) + banegård (station) + -en (the)
se sig omkring = to look around

sentences.text: Solen skinner og det er varmt.
sentences.translation: The sun is shining and it's warm.

words.grammar: en sol (a sun) · solen (the sun) · -en suffix = definite

sentences.text: Jeg har et kort i hånden.
sentences.translation: I have a map in my hand.

words.grammar: et kort · neuter noun · also means 'short' as adj.
en hånd (a hand) · hånden (the hand)

sentences.text: Først vil jeg se Nyhavn.
sentences.translation: First I want to see Nyhavn.

words.grammar: ny (new) + havn (harbor)

sentences.text: Himlen er blå i dag.
sentences.translation: The sky is blue today.

words.grammar: en himmel (a sky) · himlen (the sky) · -el drops before -en
blå · same form for all genders and plural

sentences.text: Jeg går langs kanalen og ser de farverige huse.
sentences.translation: I walk along the canal and see the colorful houses.

words.grammar: en kanal · kanalen (the canal)
farverig · farve (color) + -rig (-ful) · plural: farverige

sentences.text: København er en smuk by.
sentences.translation: Copenhagen is a beautiful city.

words.grammar: smuk · comparative: smukkere · superlative: smukkest
en by · byen (the city) · byer (cities)

sentences.text: Om aftenen spiser jeg smørrebrød.
sentences.translation: In the evening I eat smørrebrød (open-faced sandwiches).

words.grammar: en aften · aftenen (the evening) · om aftenen = in the evening
smør (butter) + brød (bread) · iconic Danish food

sentences.text: Min første dag var dejlig.
sentences.translation: My first day was lovely.

words.grammar: dejlig · one of the most-used Danish positive adjectives

sentences.text: Pippi bor alene i et gammelt hus.
sentences.translation: Pippi lives alone in an old house.

words.grammar: bo (to live/reside) · bor (present) · boede (past) · a core everyday verb, distinct from leve (to be alive)
gammel · et gammelt hus (neuter) · en gammel mand (common) · irregular: gammel – gammelt – gamle

sentences.text: Huset hedder Villakulla.
sentences.translation: The house is called Villakulla.

words.grammar: hedde (to be called) · hedder (present) · hed (past) · Hvad hedder du? = What is your name?
et hus · huset (the house) · neuter noun · husene (the houses, pl. definite)

sentences.text: Pippi har en abe og en hest.
sentences.translation: Pippi has a monkey and a horse.

words.grammar: en abe · aben (the monkey) · aber (monkeys) · aberne (the monkeys)
en hest · hesten (the horse) · heste (horses) · common gender noun

sentences.text: Hesten bor på verandaen.
sentences.translation: The horse lives on the veranda.

words.grammar: en veranda · verandaen (the veranda) · borrowed word, always common gender

sentences.text: Naboerne hedder Tommy og Annika.
sentences.translation: The neighbours are called Tommy and Annika.

words.grammar: en nabo · naboen (the neighbour) · naboer (neighbours) · naboerne (the neighbours)

sentences.text: De ser Pippi og bliver meget overraskede.
sentences.translation: They see Pippi and become very surprised.

words.grammar: overrasket (sg.) · overraskede (pl./definite) · blive overrasket = to be/become surprised · en overraskelse = a surprise
blive (to become/stay) · bliver (present) · blev (past) · very versatile: blive glad = to become happy · blive hjemme = to stay home

sentences.text: Pippi siger: "Jeg bor her nu. Vil I være mine venner?"
sentences.translation: Pippi says: 'I live here now. Will you be my friends?'

words.grammar: en ven (m.) · venner (friends, pl.) · en veninde = female friend · gode venner = good friends
ville (will/want) · I (you plural) · Vil I? = Do you (all) want to? · I is the pl. subject pronoun (informal): rarely used in modern speech, but correct

sentences.text: Tommy og Annika siger ja.
sentences.translation: Tommy and Annika say yes.

words.grammar: sige (to say) · siger (present) · sagde (past) · sagt (past participle) · Hvad siger du? = What do you say?

sentences.text: Pippi laver mad til dem begge.
sentences.translation: Pippi makes food for both of them.

words.grammar: lave (to make/do) · laver mad = to cook/make food · mad (c.) = food · laver du mad? = do you cook?
begge = both · begge to = both of them · used with plural nouns · ikke begge = not both

sentences.text: Hun er stærk og sjov – den stærkeste pige i verden!
sentences.translation: She is strong and funny – the strongest girl in the world!

words.grammar: stærk · stærkere (comparative) · stærkest/den stærkeste (superlative) · en stærk vilje = a strong will
sjov (common) · sjovt (neuter) · sjove (pl./definite) · det er sjovt = it's fun · have det sjovt = to have fun
en verden · verden (the world – already definite) · i hele verden = in the whole world

sentences.text: Pippi vil gå på markedet i dag.
sentences.translation: Pippi wants to go to the market today.

words.grammar: et marked · markedet (the market) · markeder (markets) · marked can also mean 'marked/noticed'
ville (to want/will) · vil (present) · ville (past) · Jeg vil gerne = I would like to · modal verb

sentences.text: Hun tager en stor kurv med sig.
sentences.translation: She takes a large basket with her.

words.grammar: en kurv · kurven (the basket) · kurve (baskets) · also used idiomatically: få en kurv = to be rejected (romantically)
tage med (to take along) · tager ... med · tog med (past) · Må jeg tage det med? = May I take this?

sentences.text: På markedet er der mange farver og lyde.
sentences.translation: At the market there are many colours and sounds.

words.grammar: en farve · farven (the colour) · farver (colours) · hvad er din yndlingsfarve? = what is your favourite colour?
en lyd · lyden (the sound) · lyde (sounds) · høre en lyd = to hear a sound

sentences.text: Pippi køber æbler, brød og en stor hat.
sentences.translation: Pippi buys apples, bread and a large hat.

words.grammar: købe (to buy) · køber (present) · købte (past) · købt (past participle) · regular weak verb
et brød · brødet (the bread) · brød (loaves, pl.) · brødene (the loaves) · unchanged in plural

sentences.text: En mand siger: "Piger bærer ikke så store hatte!"
sentences.translation: A man says: 'Girls don't wear such large hats!'

words.grammar: bære (to carry/wear) · bærer (present) · bar (past) · båret (past participle) · strong verb
en hat · hatten (the hat) · hatte (hats) · hattene (the hats)

sentences.text: Pippi griner og siger: "Denne pige gør det!"
sentences.translation: Pippi laughs and says: 'This girl does!'

words.grammar: grine (to laugh/grin) · griner (present) · grinede (past) · grint (past participle) · regular weak verb
denne (common gender) · dette (neuter) · disse (plural) · demonstrative pronoun · denne her = this one here (emphatic)

sentences.text: Alle på markedet griner med.
sentences.translation: Everyone at the market laughs along.

words.grammar: alle (pl.) · al (common sg.) · alt (neuter sg.) · alle sammen = everyone together · alle andre = everyone else

sentences.text: Pippi giver æbler til børnene.
sentences.translation: Pippi gives apples to the children.

words.grammar: give (to give) · giver (present) · gav (past) · givet (past participle) · strong verb · give nogen noget = to give someone something
et barn · barnet (the child) · børn (children) · børnene (the children) · irregular plural: barn – børn

sentences.text: De er glade og takker hende.
sentences.translation: They are happy and thank her.

words.grammar: glad (sg.) · glade (pl./definite) · meget glad = very happy · glad for noget = happy about something
takke (to thank) · takker (present) · takkede (past) · Tak! = Thank you! · mange tak = thank you very much

sentences.text: Pippi går hjem med den store hat på og en tom kurv.
sentences.translation: Pippi walks home with the large hat on and an empty basket.

words.grammar: tom (common) · tomt (neuter) · tomme (pl./definite) · en tom kurv = an empty basket · opposite: fuld (full)
hjem = home (motion toward) · hjemme = at home (location) · gå hjem = to go home · Jeg er hjemme = I am at home

sentences.text: Det regner udenfor.
sentences.translation: It is raining outside.

words.grammar: regne (to rain) · det regner = it is raining · regnede (past) · regnet (past participle) · impersonal verb – always used with 'det'
udenfor = outside · opposite: indenfor (inside) · can also be a preposition: udenfor huset = outside the house

sentences.text: Tommy og Annika keder sig derhjemme.
sentences.translation: Tommy and Annika are bored at home.

words.grammar: kede sig (to be bored) · reflexive verb · keder sig (present) · kedede sig (past) · Jeg keder mig = I am bored
derhjemme = at home (referring to their place) · hjemme = at home (general) · her hjemme = here at home

sentences.text: De løber over til Pippi.
sentences.translation: They run over to Pippi.

words.grammar: løbe (to run) · løber (present) · løb (past) · løbet (past participle) · strong verb
over til = over to · gå over til = to go over to · directional phrase indicating crossing a short distance

sentences.text: Pippi åbner døren og siger: "Kom ind!"
sentences.translation: Pippi opens the door and says: 'Come in!'

words.grammar: åbne (to open) · åbner (present) · åbnede (past) · åbnet (past participle) · opposite: lukke (to close)
en dør · døren (the door) · døre (doors) · dørene (the doors)

sentences.text: De sidder inde og hører på regnen.
sentences.translation: They sit inside and listen to the rain.

words.grammar: sidde (to sit) · sidder (present) · sad (past) · siddet (past participle) · strong verb
høre på (to listen to) · hører (present) · hørte (past) · distinct from lytte til (to listen attentively) · høre (to hear)

sentences.text: Pippi fortæller historier om sin far og havet.
sentences.translation: Pippi tells stories about her father and the sea.

words.grammar: fortælle (to tell/narrate) · fortæller (present) · fortalte (past) · fortalt (past participle) · irregular weak verb
et hav · havet (the sea) · have (seas) · have can also mean 'garden': en have · context distinguishes the two

sentences.text: Hendes far er kaptajn på et stort skib.
sentences.translation: Her father is a captain on a big ship.

words.grammar: en kaptajn · kaptajnen (the captain) · borrowed word from French/Dutch via English
et skib · skibet (the ship) · skibe (ships) · skibene (the ships)

sentences.text: Tommy siger: "Det lyder farligt!"
sentences.translation: Tommy says: 'That sounds dangerous!'

words.grammar: lyde (to sound) · det lyder godt = that sounds good · lød (past) · lydt (past participle) · strong verb
farlig (common) · farligt (neuter) · farlige (pl./definite) · en farlig situation = a dangerous situation

sentences.text: Pippi griner: "Nej! Det er et stort eventyr!"
sentences.translation: Pippi laughs: 'No! It is a great adventure!'

words.grammar: et eventyr · eventyret (the adventure/fairy tale) · eventyr (plural, unchanged) · H.C. Andersens eventyr = H.C. Andersen's fairy tales

sentences.text: Regnen stopper, og himlen bliver blå igen.
sentences.translation: The rain stops, and the sky becomes blue again.

words.grammar: en himmel · himlen (the sky) · the -el drops before -en suffix · himlen er blå = the sky is blue
stoppe (to stop) · stopper (present) · stoppede (past) · stoppe op = to come to a halt · loanword from English

sentences.text: De tre venner løber ud og leger.
sentences.translation: The three friends run outside and play.

words.grammar: lege (to play) · leger (present) · legede (past) · used for children's play · spille = to play sport/music/games
tre = three · used before both common and neuter nouns · tre venner = three friends

sentences.text: I Danmark er der mange grønne bakker.
sentences.translation: In Denmark there are many green hills.

words.grammar: en bakke · bakken (the hill) · bakker (hills) · bakkerne (the hills) · also means 'trays' (context-dependent)
grøn (common sg.) · grønt (neuter sg.) · grønne (pl./definite) · grøn is also used for 'eco-friendly'

sentences.text: Under bakkerne bor de underjordiske.
sentences.translation: Beneath the hills live the underground folk.

words.grammar: underjordisk (sg.) · underjordiske (pl./definite) · used as noun: de underjordiske = the hidden people · under + jord (earth) + isk
under = under/beneath · under bordet = under the table · can show location or motion · under natten = during the night

sentences.text: Om natten åbner højen sig.
sentences.translation: At night the hill opens itself.

words.grammar: en høj · højen (the hill/mound) · høje (hills) · elverhøj = elf hill · a culturally significant word in Danish folklore
om natten = at night (habitual) · i nat = tonight · natten (the night) · en nat · god nat = good night

sentences.text: Elverpigerne danser i det bløde græs.
sentences.translation: The elf maidens dance in the soft grass.

words.grammar: en elverpige · elverpigen (the elf maiden) · elverpiger (pl.) · compound: elver (elf) + pige (girl)
danse (to dance) · danser (present) · dansede (past) · danset (past participle) · regular weak verb
et græs · græsset (the grass) · no standard plural · slå græs = to mow the lawn

sentences.text: En ung mand ser lyset fra højen.
sentences.translation: A young man sees the light from the hill.

words.grammar: et lys · lyset (the light) · lys (lights, pl.) · tænde lyset = to turn on the light · also adj: lys = light/bright
ung (common) · ungt (neuter) · unge (pl./definite) · en ung mand = a young man · de unge = the young people

sentences.text: Han hører musik og føler sig underlig tiltrukket.
sentences.translation: He hears music and feels strangely drawn.

words.grammar: en musik · musikken (the music) · uncountable · spille musik = to play music
underlig (common) · underligt (neuter) · underlige (pl./definite) · en underlig følelse = a strange feeling

sentences.text: Han går nærmere og ser elverpigerne.
sentences.translation: He walks closer and sees the elf maidens.

words.grammar: nær (near) · nærmere (comparative) · nærmest (superlative) · komme nærmere = to come closer

sentences.text: De smiler og inviterer ham med i dansen.
sentences.translation: They smile and invite him to join the dance.

words.grammar: smile (to smile) · smiler (present) · smilede (past) · et smil (n.) = a smile
invitere (to invite) · inviterer (present) · inviterede (past) · en invitation (c.) = an invitation

sentences.text: Han danser hele natten og glemmer alt.
sentences.translation: He dances all night and forgets everything.

words.grammar: glemme (to forget) · glemmer (present) · glemte (past) · glemt (past participle) · irregular weak verb
hel (common/neuter sg.) · hele (definite/pl.) · hele natten = all night · hele verden = the whole world

sentences.text: Da solen kommer op, er elverpigerne væk.
sentences.translation: When the sun comes up, the elf maidens are gone.

words.grammar: en sol · solen (the sun) · sole (suns, rare) · solen skinner = the sun shines · solskin (n.) = sunshine
væk = gone/away · de er væk = they are gone · gå væk = go away · forsvinde = to disappear

sentences.text: Manden står alene på den grønne bakke.
sentences.translation: The man stands alone on the green hill.

words.grammar: stå (to stand) · står (present) · stod (past) · stået (past participle) · strong verb

sentences.text: Han husker dansen, men han ved ikke om det var en drøm.
sentences.translation: He remembers the dance, but he does not know if it was a dream.

words.grammar: huske (to remember) · husker (present) · huskede (past) · Kan du huske? = Do you remember?
en drøm · drømmen (the dream) · drømme (dreams) · drømme (to dream) – same form as the noun plural
